prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) were computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file as-is. Partial charges were computed via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF) for the neutral (N), cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states.

Method Provenance: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted), PySCF RKS, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Fukui indices derived from finite-difference Mulliken charges.

Results (per atom, 1-based indexing):
  Atom 1 (O):  f+ = 0.46814,  f- = 0.46742,  f0 = 0.46778,  dual = +0.00071
  Atom 2 (C):  f+ = 0.70839,  f- = 0.11244,  f0 = 0.41042,  dual = +0.59595
  Atom 3 (H):  f+ = -0.08827, f- = 0.21008,  f0 = 0.06090,  dual = -0.29835
  Atom 4 (H):  f+ = -0.08826, f- = 0.21006,  f0 = 0.06090,  dual = -0.29832

Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f+ = 0.70839 (headline value reported).
Most nucleophilic site:  O (atom 1), f- = 0.46742.

The carbonyl carbon (C) is the dominant electrophilic site—consistent with formaldehyde's well-known susceptibility to nucleophilic addition. The carbonyl oxygen (O) is the dominant nucleophilic site. Negative f+ values on H atoms are a Mulliken-scheme artifact.

No warnings were reported by the engine. Integrity checks passed: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000, schema_header present. integrity.trustworthy = true.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz
Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-175534_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
